topic: Cursor 3 vs Google Antigravity: Which AI IDE Wins in 2026?

competitors.title: Cursor 3 vs Google Antigravity: Best AI IDE 2026
key_points: Cursor 3 launched April 2, 2026 with Agents Window - standalone interface for managing multiple AI agents in parallel
Cursor crossed $2 billion annual revenue early 2026, doubling in three months, 25% market share among generative AI software buyers
Over 50% of Fortune 500 companies adopted Cursor by mid-2025 (Nvidia, Uber, Adobe)
Google Antigravity released November 2025 as free agent-first IDE, powered by Gemini 3.1 Pro and Claude Opus 4.6
Antigravity scores 76.2% on SWE-bench Verified and 54.2% on Terminal-Bench 2.0
Antigravity has 2 million token context window - entire codebase in context at once
Artifacts system creates verifiable records of agent actions for compliance and debugging
Manager View allows dispatch of 5 parallel agents with real-time monitoring
Antigravity lacks MCP support currently, while Cursor has marketplace with hundreds of plugins
Cursor Pro is $20/month with unlimited Composer 2, Antigravity is free with Gemini 3.1 Pro and Claude Opus 4.6

competitors.title: Cursor vs Google Antigravity (2026): Which Is Better? Complete Comparison
key_points: Both tools offer free tiers
Cursor best for professional developers working on complex, multi-file projects
Google Antigravity best for developers seeking free AI coding assistance without subscription costs
Cursor has web app, Antigravity has web app plus API access
Cursor features natural language editing and AI code interaction
Antigravity features include desktop app, integrations, collaboration, export options
Antigravity is agent-first IDE built as Visual Studio Code fork
Antigravity powered by Gemini 3 Pro with 1 million token context window
Antigravity supports multi-model: Claude Sonnet 4.5, Opus 4.5, GPT-OSS
Antigravity has Editor View for coding, Manager View for orchestrating multiple agents

stats.fact: Cursor crossed $2 billion annual revenue early 2026, doubling in three months
Over 50% of Fortune 500 companies adopted Cursor by mid-2025
Google Antigravity scores 76.2% on SWE-bench Verified
Antigravity has 2 million token context window - entire codebase in context at once
Devin, 2024's 'autonomous software engineer', scored 13.86% on SWE-bench at launch
Claude Code with Opus 4.6 scores approximately 72% on SWE-bench Verified
Antigravity is completely free with Gemini 3.1 Pro and Claude Opus 4.6 included

angles: Pricing disruption: Free tier with Opus 4.6 access puts pressure on paid coding assistants
Architectural philosophy: Cursor's Agents Window vs Antigravity's Manager View vs Claude Code's terminal-native approach
Enterprise compliance: Artifacts system for verifiable records vs traditional code review
Context window advantage: 2M tokens enable whole-codebase reasoning vs project embeddings
Ecosystem integration: MCP marketplace (Cursor) vs Google ecosystem (Antigravity)
outline: Introduction: The AI IDE race intensifies in 2026
What is Cursor 3? Agents Window and architectural shift
What is Google Antigravity? Free agent-first IDE with Gemini 3
Feature breakdown: Agents Window vs Manager View vs Artifacts
Context window advantage: 2M tokens vs project embeddings
Pricing analysis: Cursor Pro $20 vs Antigravity free vs Claude Code API
Market position: $2B revenue vs free disruption
Ecosystem integration: MCP plugins vs Google ecosystem
Enterprise adoption: Fortune 500 adoption vs compliance needs
Stability considerations: Maturity vs reliability trade-offs
Multi-agent workflow revolution: Parallel execution productivity
Future outlook: Where AI IDEs go from 2026
Conclusion: Which AI coding IDE should you choose?
